title: Project scaffold + foundation types
description: Create project scaffold: package.json, tsconfig.json, biome.json, src/index.ts (entry point + VERSION), src/types.ts (ALL shared types/interfaces from spec), src/errors.ts (SaplingError hierarchy), src/config.ts (config loader + defaults), src/json.ts (JSON envelope helpers), src/logging/logger.ts + color.ts, src/test-helpers.ts. Verify quality gates pass (bun test, biome check, tsc --noEmit). File area: root config files + src/types.ts, src/errors.ts, src/config.ts, src/json.ts, src/logging/*, src/test-helpers.ts
closeReason: Project scaffold complete: 14 files (package.json, tsconfig.json, biome.json, bun.lock + 10 src/ files). All foundation types, errors, config, JSON helpers, logging, and test helpers implemented per spec. 14 tests passing, lint and typecheck clean.

title: Tool system - 6 tools + registry
description: Implement all 6 tools (bash, read, write, edit, grep, glob) + tool registry + dispatch. Each tool implements Tool interface with execute() and toDefinition(). Tests colocated using real filesystem (temp dirs). ~800 lines estimated. File area: src/tools/*
closeReason: Implemented complete tool system: 6 tools (bash, read, write, edit, grep, glob) + ToolRegistry + dispatch. 23 files, 1562 lines, 50 tests all passing. Quality gates clean (tests, lint, typecheck). Commit b638726 on branch overstory/tools-builder/sapling-e469.

title: LLM client - CC subprocess + Anthropic SDK backends
description: Implement LlmClient interface with two backends: 1) CC subprocess (primary, subscription billing) using claude -p with --tools '' --max-turns 1 --output-format json --json-schema, 2) Raw Anthropic SDK (secondary, API billing) using @anthropic-ai/sdk. Both return LlmResponse with content blocks + usage. Include token estimation. ~400 lines estimated. File area: src/client/*
closeReason: Implemented LlmClient interface with CC subprocess backend (CcClient) and Anthropic SDK backend (AnthropicClient). Both implement estimateTokens, return LlmResponse with content blocks + TokenUsage. CC backend uses claude -p with --tools '' --max-turns 1 --output-format json --json-schema. Anthropic backend uses @anthropic-ai/sdk with DI for testability. 50 tests, all gates passing.

title: Context manager - measure/score/prune/archive/reshape pipeline
description: Implement the core context management pipeline: measure.ts (token counting + budget tracking), score.ts (relevance scoring per message with recency/file-overlap/error-context/decision/size signals), prune.ts (pruning strategies for tool results and assistant messages), archive.ts (working memory with workSummary/decisions/modifiedFiles/resolvedErrors), reshape.ts (rebuild messages array), manager.ts (orchestrates pipeline). Tests for each stage. ~800 lines estimated. File area: src/context/*
closeReason: Context manager pipeline implemented: measure (token counting + budget), score (5-signal relevance), prune (bash truncation, stale reads, summarization, score-based), archive (working memory), reshape (message array rebuild), manager (orchestrator). 18 files, 2657 lines, 67 tests passing, lint+typecheck clean.

title: Agent loop + CLI integration
description: Implement the agent turn loop (src/loop.ts): LLM call -> tool dispatch -> context management -> repeat. Parallel tool execution via Promise.all. Stop conditions (no tool calls, max turns, unrecoverable). Error handling with exponential backoff. Wire CLI commands in src/index.ts via Commander.js (sapling run, sapling version). ~300 lines + CLI wiring. Depends on scaffold, tools, client, and context manager. File area: src/loop.ts, src/loop.test.ts, CLI parts of src/index.ts, agents/*.md
closeReason: Implemented agent turn loop (src/loop.ts) with parallel tool execution via Promise.all, all stop conditions (task_complete, max_turns, error), exponential backoff retry for LLM errors, and context manager integration. Created CLI entry point (src/index.ts) with Commander.js supporting all flags (--model, --cwd, --backend, --system-prompt-file, --max-turns, --verbose, --json, --quiet). CLI run handler (src/cli.ts) with stub factories marked for merge-time integration. Agent definitions (agents/builder.md, scout.md, reviewer.md). 13 tests passing, lint clean, typecheck clean.

title: Integration verification and cross-module wiring
description: All 5 Sapling MVP components were built in parallel worktrees and merged to main via auto-resolve. Need to: 1) Run bun install to get dependencies. 2) Run quality gates (bun test, biome check, tsc --noEmit). 3) Fix any import mismatches between modules — each component built its own scaffold stubs. Key files to check: src/types.ts vs src/tools/types.ts vs src/client/types.ts, src/errors.ts imports across all modules, src/index.ts CLI wiring to src/cli.ts and src/loop.ts. 4) Wire the CLI run command to the real loop + client + tools + context manager. 5) All quality gates must pass.
closeReason: Integration verification complete. Consolidated duplicate types into canonical src/types.ts, replaced stubs with real wiring (AnthropicClient, CcClient, createDefaultRegistry, createContextManager), wired CLI run command, added mock test helpers. 164 tests pass, lint clean, typecheck clean.

title: Integration v2: fix cross-module types and wiring on merged main
description: The Sapling MVP was built by 5 parallel leads and all merged to main. An integration pass was done but its branch could not merge due to conflicts. Need to redo integration on current main: 1) bun install. 2) tsc --noEmit to find type errors. 3) Fix all cross-module import/type mismatches. Key issues to expect: missing types in src/types.ts (LlmBackend, SaplingConfig, ContextBudget, BudgetUtilization, ContextArchive, ContextManager, Tool, ToolRegistry interface, ToolResult, ScoredMessage, MessageCategory, RunOptions), duplicate types in src/client/types.ts and src/tools/types.ts (should re-export from canonical src/types.ts), ToolRegistry.definitions() should be renamed to toDefinitions(), test helpers need mock client helpers, src/cli.ts has TODO stubs that need real wiring, src/index.ts run command needs real CLI handler. Reference the failed integration branch overstory/integration-builder/sapling-11b1 for what changes were needed. All quality gates must pass: bun test, biome check src/, tsc --noEmit.
closeReason: Integration v2 complete: consolidated 13 types into src/types.ts, replaced duplicate type defs in client/types.ts and tools/types.ts with re-exports, wired real client/tool/context modules in cli.ts, implemented run command in index.ts, added test helpers. All quality gates pass (164 tests, clean lint, zero type errors).

title: Benchmarking harness: validate context pipeline vs CC/Pi
description: Step 7 from the MVP spec. Build a benchmarking harness to compare Sapling's token usage against CC/Pi on equivalent tasks. Success criteria from spec: (1) Total token usage 30-50% less than CC/Pi on equivalent tasks, (2) Agent never hits context limit unexpectedly, (3) Coherence doesn't degrade after pruning. This also resolves open questions 1-4: CC structured output fidelity, subprocess latency at scale, summarization quality, and budget tuning.
closeReason: Benchmarking harness created in src/bench/ with 3 scenarios (10/30/100 turns), runner comparing baseline vs managed modes, 48 tests. Long scenario achieves >=30% token reduction.

title: Overstory Phase 1: Basic runtime adapter
description: First phase of Overstory integration (per spec). Build src/runtimes/sapling.ts in overstory that implements the AgentRuntime interface. Key requirements: (1) Spawn Sapling as subprocess via Bun.spawn, (2) Pass task + system prompt via CLI flags, (3) Read NDJSON events from stdout, (4) Check process liveness via kill(pid, 0) — no tmux, (5) Session store uses pid instead of tmux_session. This makes Sapling a first-class agent runtime alongside Claude, Pi, and Codex in Overstory.

title: Overstory Phase 2: JSON-RPC protocol
description: Second phase of Overstory integration (per spec). Sapling listens on stdin for JSON-RPC commands. Supports: start, steer, followUp, abort. Implements RuntimeConnection interface. Enables mid-task mail injection and course correction from Overstory.

title: Overstory Phase 3: Ecosystem integration (mulch, seeds, mail, metrics)
description: Third phase of Overstory integration (per spec). Wire Sapling into the os-eco ecosystem: (1) Direct bun:sqlite access to mail.db for between-turn mail checks, (2) mulch prime on startup for domain expertise, (3) sd update as work progresses, (4) Real-time metrics writes to metrics.db (WAL mode, 5s busy timeout).

title: Overstory Phase 4: Guard system
description: Fourth phase of Overstory integration (per spec). Implement TypeScript guard functions (not shell scripts): (1) Path boundary enforcement, (2) Capability-based tool restrictions, (3) Bash command filtering. Guards are invoked by the agent loop before tool execution.

title: Context pipeline tuning: improve scoring, pruning, and archive quality
description: Ongoing improvement of the context pipeline based on real-world usage. The current implementation uses conservative defaults (template-based summaries, 4-char/token heuristic, 15/10/40/15/20 budget split). As we dogfood Sapling, tune based on observed behavior: (1) Scoring weight adjustments (recency 0.30, file overlap 0.25, error 0.20, decision 0.15, size 0.10), (2) Pruning strategy refinement — when does template-based summarization lose too much?, (3) Archive quality — wire up file hash staleness detection (computed but unused), (4) Budget tuning with real data, (5) Evaluate LLM-based summarization (haiku) when template-based falls short. This is a living issue — update as dogfooding reveals specific gaps.
closeReason: Context pipeline tuning: fileHashes wired up with Bun.hash and fingerprint staleness detection, scoring rebalanced with unresolved question signal, grep output summarization and user tool result compression. 9 new tests.

title: SDK backend as primary: dogfood via Pi to avoid nested CC sessions
description: CC subprocess backend cannot be used when Sapling is invoked from within another CC session (Pi, Claude Code) — nested CC sessions are not allowed. For dogfooding, the SDK backend (AnthropicClient) must be the primary path. Ensure the SDK backend is robust for daily use: (1) Verify SDK backend works reliably for multi-turn tasks, (2) Confirm error handling and retry logic matches CC backend quality, (3) Document the Pi invocation pattern (sp run --backend sdk), (4) Consider making backend auto-detect whether it's inside a CC session.
closeReason: SDK backend now auto-detects CC sessions via CLAUDECODE env var, has error classification for retry logic (5 HTTP status codes mapped), DI support for testing, and updated UNRECOVERABLE_CODES. grep schema also fixed (sapling-1f98).

title: Wire logger initialization from CLI flags (--verbose, --quiet)
description: The Logger class and configure() function exist in src/logging/logger.ts but are never initialized from CLI flags. After loadConfig() in src/index.ts, call configure({ verbose, quiet, json }) so the logger respects --verbose (debug level), --quiet (error-only), and --json (NDJSON mode). Also call setColorEnabled(false) from src/logging/color.ts when quiet mode is active.
closeReason: Called configure({ verbose, quiet, json }) and setColorEnabled(false) in src/index.ts after loadConfig(). Logger respects --verbose, --quiet, and --json flags.

title: Wire --json flag to produce JSON envelope output
description: The --json CLI flag is parsed in src/index.ts but never used. src/json.ts has jsonOutput(), jsonError(), printJson(), printJsonError() helpers that are never imported anywhere. Add json field to RunOptions and SaplingConfig in src/types.ts, add json:false default in src/config.ts, and when --json is active: output LoopResult via printJson() instead of human-readable summary, and use printJsonError() on error paths.
closeReason: Added json field to SaplingConfig and RunOptions, json:false default in config, wired --json through loadConfig, output result via printJson/printJsonError when active.

title: Replace direct console.log/console.error calls with logger
description:
Five places bypass the logging layer with direct console calls:
1. src/index.ts:49 — console.error() for final summary → use logger.info()
2. src/index.ts:64 — console.log(VERSION) for version command → use logger or printJson
3. src/loop.ts:206 — process.stdout.write(finalText) for agent output → use logger.info()
4. src/context/manager.ts:297 — console.error() for metrics → use logger.debug()
All output should flow through src/logging/logger.ts so --quiet, --verbose, and --json modes work consistently.
closeReason: Replaced console.error in context/manager.ts logMetrics with logger.debug. Replaced console.error summary in index.ts with logger.info. Changed loop.ts finalText to logger.info (note: may conflict with mx-85b033 convention).

title: CC client reads 'result' instead of 'structured_output' from claude CLI response
description: The CcClient parses raw.result from the claude CLI JSON response, but the current claude CLI puts the JSON schema response in raw.structured_output, not raw.result. raw.result contains the plain text summary. This causes structured tool_calls to be completely missed — the client sees no tool calls and treats every response as task_complete after 1 turn. Evidence: claude CLI returns { result: 'Created hello.txt...', structured_output: { thinking: '...', tool_calls: [...] } }. CcClient reads raw.result (plain text), tries JSON.parse, fails, falls back to { thinking: '', text_response: raw.result }. Loop sees 0 tool_use blocks, reports task_complete after 1 turn with no actual work done. Fix: Update CcRawResponse to include structured_output field, read it first, fall back to result. This is the PRIMARY blocker — the CC backend is completely non-functional for tool-using tasks.
closeReason: CcClient now reads structured_output field from claude CLI response, with fallback to result for backward compat. Fixes critical bug where tool_calls were completely missed.

title: CC client tool name mismatch: CC returns capitalized names, Sapling registers lowercase
description: When using the CC backend with --json-schema, the LLM inside Claude Code returns tool calls using CC's built-in tool names (Write, Read, Edit, Bash, Glob) instead of Sapling's lowercase names (write, read, edit, bash, glob). The ToolRegistry.get() lookup is case-sensitive and returns undefined, causing 'Tool not found' errors. Evidence: structured_output from claude CLI contains { tool_calls: [{ name: 'Write', input: { path: '...', content: '...' } }] } but Sapling registers tools as 'write', 'read', etc. Fix options: (1) Normalize tool names to lowercase in the CC client before returning, (2) Make ToolRegistry case-insensitive, (3) Include explicit Sapling tool definitions in the system prompt/CC prompt so the LLM uses correct names. Option 3 is preferred as it also fixes the input schema mismatch (CC uses 'path', Sapling uses 'file_path').
closeReason: CcClient now normalizes tool names to lowercase via tc.name.toLowerCase() when building tool_use blocks. Added system prompt directive requiring exact lowercase names. 4 new tests cover name normalization. Quality gates pass (277 tests, typecheck clean, lint clean).

title: CC client does not pass Sapling tool definitions to the LLM
description: The CC backend passes --tools '' to disable CC's built-in tools, then uses --json-schema to get structured tool_calls back. However, Sapling's actual tool names and input schemas are never communicated to the LLM. The system prompt says 'You have access to tools for reading and writing files' but doesn't list tool names or schemas. The tool definitions from request.tools are ignored by the CC client. Result: the LLM guesses tool names (using CC's built-in names like Write/Read) and input schemas (e.g. 'path' instead of 'file_path'). Fix: Include Sapling tool definitions in the system prompt or user prompt sent to the CC subprocess. The builder.md agent prompt already lists tools by name — the CC client should also inject the full tool schemas. Related to sapling-48ad (tool name mismatch).
closeReason: CcClient now injects Sapling tool definitions (name, description, input_schema) into system prompt when tools provided. LLM uses correct tool names and schemas.

title: verbose flag not wired to logger: debug logs never appear
description: The logger module exports a configure() function that accepts { verbose: true } to enable debug-level logging. However, configure() is never imported or called anywhere in the codebase. The --verbose CLI flag sets config.verbose=true, which is passed to the context manager (for its own logging), but the global logger remains at the default info level. Debug logs in CcClient (Spawning CC subprocess, CC raw response, CC subprocess exited) and in the agent loop are never visible. Fix: Call configure({ verbose: config.verbose, quiet: config.quiet }) in the CLI entry point (src/index.ts or src/cli.ts) before creating the client/loop.
closeReason: Wired configure({ verbose, quiet, json }) in src/index.ts after loadConfig(). When --verbose is passed, logger level is set to debug.

title: grep tool case_insensitive schema declares string type instead of boolean
description: In src/tools/grep.ts, the inputSchema for case_insensitive has type: 'string' but the runtime check handles both boolean true and string 'true'. The schema should declare type: 'boolean' so the LLM knows to pass a boolean value. The current schema misleads the LLM into potentially passing arbitrary strings. Minor but causes inconsistency between schema declaration and runtime behavior.
closeReason: Fixed case_insensitive schema type from string to boolean in grep tool

title: reshapeMessages can produce consecutive user messages violating Anthropic API
description: reshapeMessages() produces [task(user)] + [archive(user)] + [history...] + [current...]. When the archive is non-empty, this creates two consecutive user messages (task then archive). The Anthropic Messages API requires strictly alternating user/assistant roles. The SDK backend (AnthropicClient) passes request.messages directly to client.messages.create(), so this would cause an API error once the context manager starts archiving. The CC backend avoids this by serializing everything to plain text. Fix options: (1) Merge archive content into the task message, (2) Insert a synthetic assistant acknowledgment between task and archive, (3) Make the archive a system-level injection rather than a user message.
closeReason: Fixed consecutive user messages: synthetic assistant ack inserted between task and archive in reshapeMessages. 2 new tests.

title: CC test mock uses stale response format: structured data in result instead of structured_output
description: The CC client tests in src/client/cc.test.ts use makeCcOutput() which puts the structured JSON data inside the 'result' field as a JSON string: { type: 'result', result: JSON.stringify(structured), ... }. However, the current claude CLI puts structured JSON in a separate 'structured_output' field and plain text in 'result'. The tests pass because they match the current (broken) parsing code. When the parsing is fixed (sapling-501a), these tests will need to be updated to use the correct response format with structured_output. Fix: Update makeCcOutput() to emit { result: 'plain text summary', structured_output: { thinking: '...', tool_calls: [...] } } format.
closeReason: Already fixed: makeCcOutput() in cc.test.ts already uses structured_output field with plain text in result. makeCcOutputLegacy() correctly tests the legacy path. No changes needed.

title: CLAUDE.md documents --max-turns 1 for CC client but flag does not exist and is not passed
description: CLAUDE.md states: 'CcClient (cc.ts, default) - spawns claude subprocess with --max-turns 1'. However: (1) The claude CLI does not have a --max-turns flag (confirmed via --help), (2) The CcClient code does not attempt to pass any such flag. Without turn limiting, the claude subprocess runs multiple internal turns (observed num_turns: 2-3 in responses), potentially executing its own tools before Sapling sees the response. With --tools '' this mostly prevents actual tool execution, but it means extra API cost and latency for wasted internal CC turns. The CLAUDE.md documentation should be corrected. Consider if there is an alternative way to limit CC to a single API call.
closeReason: Corrected CLAUDE.md to remove false --max-turns 1 reference. Documented actual CcClient flags: --output-format json, --json-schema.

title: Context pipeline pruning is a no-op: all history messages miscategorized as task/current
description: In manager.ts line 129, scoreMessages is called with currentTurnIdx=0: scoreMessages(historyMessages, this.currentFiles, 0). Inside scoreMessages, categorizeMessage is called with this 0 as currentTurnStart. With currentTurnStart=0: index 0 gets category 'task', all other indices get 'current' (because index >= 0). No message ever gets category 'history'. In pruneMessage, both 'task' and 'current' categories are immediately returned without any pruning. This means score-based pruning (summarize at score<0.35, drop at score<0.15) NEVER fires. The context pipeline measurement and scoring runs, but the actual pruning is completely inert. The context window will fill up in long tasks and the agent will hit token limits without any graceful degradation. Fix: Pass the correct currentTurnIdx from the full message array, or since historyMessages are already known to be history, categorize them all as 'history' directly.
closeReason: Fixed pruning no-op: categorizeMessage now correctly categorizes history messages, scoreMessages passes historyMessages.length as currentTurnIdx. 4 new tests.

title: pruneMessages index misalignment: kept array indices do not match scoredMessages
description: In pruneMessages (src/context/prune.ts), after filtering dropped messages: const kept = results.filter(r => !r.wasDropped).map(r => r.message). Then historyMessages is computed using: kept.filter((_, i) => { const scored = scoredMessages[i]; return scored.category === 'history'; }). Since 'kept' has had dropped items removed, kept[i] no longer corresponds to scoredMessages[i]. Example: if scoredMessages has 10 items and items 3 and 7 were dropped, kept has 8 items but kept[5] maps to scoredMessages[6] not scoredMessages[5]. This causes incorrect budget enforcement. Currently masked by sapling-7c7d (nothing is categorized as history), but will become visible once that bug is fixed. Fix: Either maintain the original index mapping, or build kept as a parallel array that preserves the association.
closeReason: Fixed pruneMessages index misalignment: maintained {result, scored} pairing through dropped-message filtering so kept[i] correctly maps to its original scored entry. Also fixed scoreMap to map pruned messages (not originals) to scores. 2 regression tests added. All 274 tests pass.

title: findToolResult never matches tool_result blocks due to type mismatch
description: In src/context/archive.ts, findToolResult() is supposed to find tool result content for a given tool_use ID. However: (1) It checks block.type === 'tool_use' instead of 'tool_result', (2) Tool results use 'tool_use_id' field, not 'id', (3) ContentBlock union type only has 'text' and 'tool_use' — 'tool_result' is defined separately in loop.ts as ToolResultBlock and cast via 'as Message[]'. The function always falls through to the weak heuristic that returns the first text block from any user message. This means turn summaries in the archive have incorrect outcomes. For example, summarizeToolCall may report '?' for outcomes or associate the wrong result with a tool call. Fix: Either add tool_result to the ContentBlock union type, or use a different approach to associate tool calls with their results (e.g., by message ordering since tool results always follow tool calls).
closeReason: Fixed findToolResult type checks: checks tool_result/tool_use_id instead of tool_use/id. ToolResultBlock exported from types.ts. 30 new tests in archive.test.ts.

title: Apply forest palette brand color rgb(76, 175, 80)
description: Add branded RGB color to logging/color.ts. Brand: chalk.rgb(76, 175, 80), accent: chalk.rgb(255, 183, 77), muted: chalk.rgb(120, 120, 110). See branding/visual-spec.md for full palette spec.
closeReason: Brand palette added to src/logging/color.ts: primary rgb(76,175,80), accent rgb(255,183,77), muted rgb(120,120,110)

title: Missing system-prompt-file shows raw ENOENT stack trace instead of a friendly error message
ConfigError for invalid backend shows raw stack trace instead of friendly error message
max-turns 0 silently ignored due to falsy check in config merge, falls back to 200
SDK auth resolution error retries 3 times instead of aborting immediately - not classified as unrecoverable
closeReason: Fixed SDK auth resolution retry: classifySdkError() now detects missing API key errors by message pattern and classifies as SDK_AUTH_FAILED (unrecoverable), preventing 3 unnecessary retries

title: Fish shell completions have incorrect logic: 'not A; or B' should be a single __fish_seen_subcommand_from call
closeReason: Fish completions fixed: replaced broken 'not A; or B' pattern with single __fish_seen_subcommand_from call with all subcommands as args.

title: README and CLAUDE.md cite stale test counts (279/19/921) vs actual (298/24/959)
closeReason: Updated stale test counts (279/19/921 → 298/24/959) in CLAUDE.md, README.md, and CHANGELOG.md

title: CLAUDE.md documents stale scoring weights (5 signals) but code uses 6 signals with different values
closeReason: Fixed stale scoring weights in CLAUDE.md: 5 signals → 6 signals matching score.ts (added unresolved question 0.08, adjusted decision 0.12, size 0.05)

title: BashTool has pipe deadlock risk: awaits proc.exited before draining stdout/stderr (fixed in CcClient)
closeReason: Fixed BashTool pipe deadlock. Changed to drain stdout/stderr concurrently with proc.exited via Promise.all. Added 200KB regression test. All tests pass.

title: CLAUDE.md says 14 benchmark scenarios but only 3 exist (SHORT, MEDIUM, LONG)
closeReason: Fixed stale benchmark scenario count in CLAUDE.md: 14 → 3 (SHORT/MEDIUM/LONG)

title: CcClient subprocess has no timeout - can hang indefinitely on invalid model or stalled API call
closeReason: Added configurable subprocess timeout to CcClient (default 120s). On timeout, kills subprocess and throws CC_TIMEOUT. Prevents indefinite hangs on invalid model or stalled API calls.

title: CC backend tool_result serialization broken — agent cannot use tools
description: serializeContentBlock() in src/client/cc.ts only handles 'text' and 'tool_use' ContentBlock types. When loop.ts pushes ToolResultBlock[] (type: 'tool_result') as user message content after tool execution, the serializer falls through to the else branch and produces '[Tool Call: undefined(undefined)]' because ToolResultBlock has no .name or .input fields. The LLM never sees actual tool output, causing it to hallucinate task completion. This makes the CC backend (default) completely non-functional for any multi-turn tool-using task. Repro: sp run 'Create hello.ts with a greet function' --verbose — file is never created despite agent claiming success.

title: LLM thinking text leaks into user-visible output
description: The CC structured response includes a 'thinking' field which is added as a text ContentBlock in cc.ts. When loop.ts extracts the final text response, it concatenates ALL text blocks including the thinking block and prints them to stdout. Example output: 'The user just wants a simple greeting. No tools needed.\nHello! I'm Sapling...' The thinking text should be suppressed from output (or only shown with --verbose). This affects --quiet mode too — thinking text is still printed even when --quiet is set. Fix: either filter out the thinking text block before printing, or tag it with metadata so loop.ts can distinguish thinking from response.

title: max-turns accepts non-numeric values causing immediate exit
description: Running sp run test --max-turns abc results in maxTurns=NaN which passes config validation because NaN < 1 is false in JavaScript. The agent loop immediately exits with max turns NaN reached after 0 turns. Fix: add Number.isNaN check in validateConfig before the < 1 comparison.

title: Invalid cwd path causes misleading posix_spawn error with retries
description: Running sp run test --cwd /nonexistent/dir gives the error ENOENT: no such file or directory, posix_spawn claude with 3 retries and exponential backoff (~4s wasted). The cwd should be validated to exist before entering the agent loop. Fix: add directory existence check in validateConfig or runCommand, throwing a clear ConfigError like Working directory not found.

title: Empty prompt accepted without validation
description: Running sp run with an empty string prompt is accepted and processed, making an LLM call that produces a generic greeting response and burns tokens. Should validate that prompt is non-empty and reject with a clear error. Fix: add prompt validation in the run command handler or CLI option parser.

title: No stdin/pipe support for prompt input
description: Cannot pipe a prompt via stdin, e.g. echo task | sp run or cat prompt.txt | sp run. Commander requires the prompt as a positional argument. This limits composability with other CLI tools. Consider reading from stdin when no positional prompt is provided and stdin is not a TTY.

title: JSON output mixed with plain text in quiet and json modes
description: When running with --quiet --json, the final text response is printed as plain text to stdout alongside the JSON envelope. Example: thinking text and response text appear before the JSON object. In --json mode, all output should be structured JSON events. The final response text should be a JSON event, not raw text. Similarly --quiet should suppress all non-essential output including the thinking text.

title: CC backend creates new subprocess per turn losing conversational context
description: Each turn in the agent loop spawns a fresh claude subprocess with the entire conversation serialized into the -p flag. This means: 1) Every turn pays full input token cost for the entire history (no caching benefit). 2) The serialization format [User]: ... [Assistant]: ... is a lossy approximation of the real message structure. 3) Combined with the tool_result serialization bug (sapling-49de), the LLM has very poor understanding of what happened in prior turns. Consider using claude session persistence or restructuring the CC backend to maintain a single session.

title: WriteTool uses absolute paths only, ignores cwd parameter
description: WriteTool.execute() receives a cwd parameter but ignores it. The tool description says 'Absolute path to the file to write' but the LLM may provide a relative path. When the LLM uses a relative path, the file is written relative to the process cwd (where sapling was invoked), not the --cwd passed to the run command. Fix: resolve file_path against cwd when it is not absolute, like: const resolved = path.isAbsolute(filePath) ? filePath : path.resolve(cwd, filePath).

title: GrepTool pipe deadlock: awaits proc.exited before draining stdout
description: GrepTool in src/tools/grep.ts calls await proc.exited before reading stdout/stderr. If ripgrep output exceeds the OS pipe buffer (~65KB), rg blocks on write while sapling blocks on proc.exited, causing a deadlock. This is the exact pattern fixed in BashTool and CcClient (see mulch failures mx-f56cc1, mx-c64976). Fix: use Promise.all([proc.exited, new Response(proc.stdout).text(), new Response(proc.stderr).text()]) as done in bash.ts.

title: Read, Write, and Edit tools ignore cwd parameter for relative paths
description: ReadTool, WriteTool, and EditTool all accept a cwd parameter in execute() but ignore it (param named _cwd). Their input schemas require 'Absolute path' but the LLM may provide relative paths, especially for simple tasks. When a relative path is used, the file operation targets process.cwd() not the --cwd directory. BashTool and GrepTool correctly use cwd. Fix: resolve file_path against cwd when not absolute using path.resolve(cwd, filePath) in all three tools.

title: SAPLING_MODEL env var never takes effect due to CLI default override
description: The run command CLI option --model has a Commander default of claude-sonnet-4-6. This means opts.model is always truthy and always included in the loadConfig overrides. Since loadConfig spreads overrides AFTER env vars ({ ...fromEnv, ...overrides }), the CLI default always wins over SAPLING_MODEL. Repro: SAPLING_MODEL=claude-haiku-3 sp run hi --max-turns 1 -- log shows claude-sonnet-4-6. Same issue applies to SAPLING_BACKEND and SAPLING_MAX_TURNS. Fix: only include CLI values in overrides when explicitly provided by the user, not when they are Commander defaults.

title: Timing output includes ANSI escape codes when stderr is piped
description: The --timing flag outputs elapsed time via colors.dim() to stderr. When stderr is redirected or piped, ANSI escape codes are still included in the output. The setColorEnabled() call only runs during the run command action and applies to logger output, but the timing output uses colors.dim() directly in index.ts after the logger is configured. When --json is set, setColorEnabled(false) is called but the timing output still gets ANSI codes because it reads colors at write time. This should respect TTY detection on stderr.
